body:
## Summary
- Build the agent runtime before posting unsupported-route responses.
- Keeps unsupported replies direct and avoids invoking the answer agent.
- Adds workflow coverage so unsupported responses have runtime setup before calling post-response.js.

## Verification
- npm --prefix .agent ci
- npm --prefix .agent test

comments.body:
## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 93 | PARTIAL | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Change reuses existing `Setup agent runtime` action by widening its `if` condition instead of adding new runtime logic; test extends existing envelope assertions. ([agent-router.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/47f14a0609c816ac3ca354fc39e048c2653bc019/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml), [.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/47f14a0609c816ac3ca354fc39e048c2653bc019/.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts)) |
| workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR metadata/body has no parent issue/plan references to follow up; scope is self-contained and aligned with existing unsupported-route behavior summary in docs. |
| workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Fix ensures unsupported path gets runtime setup before `post-response.js` (prevents state/runtime-dependent posting failures) and adds a regression assertion for this exact condition. |
| workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | partial | 4/7 | Workflow/runtime behavior was adjusted, but no docs changed in this PR. Existing docs still describe unsupported inline replies, so mismatch risk is low but non-zero. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Only 2 files touched, with minimal targeted edits (condition widening + corresponding test coverage). |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Simplest viable fix: broaden a single `if` expression and add one focused test assertion. |

## Notes

- Strong rubric alignment on reuse, simplicity, and surgical scope; implementation is minimal and test-backed.
- Main score reduction is documentation sync: behavior changed in workflow internals without an explicit docs touch.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `keep-docs-in-sync` is only partially satisfied; consider a brief note in workflow/request-lifecycle docs that unsupported-route posting now also runs runtime setup before `post-response.js`.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR fixes a workflow bug where unsupported-route responses could fail because the agent runtime was not built before calling `post-response.js`.
- The change broadens the `Setup agent runtime` condition in the `answer` job to run for both `answer` and `unsupported` routes.
- It also adds regression coverage in the envelope workflow test to assert runtime setup is present for unsupported responses.
- The goal is to keep unsupported replies direct (no answer-agent handoff) while ensuring the required `.agent/dist` runtime exists.
- Scope is small and targeted to workflow gating plus test coverage.

## Review
- Core implementation looks correct and surgical, and two reviews found no functional regressions.
- One reviewer raised a docs-sync concern: behavior in workflow internals changed, but no accompanying doc note was added.
- No new inline PR comment was posted because this concern is already captured in existing PR discussion and is not a line-specific defect.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Docs note for unsupported-route runtime setup is missing | WARNING | Workflow behavior now explicitly builds runtime before unsupported posting, but docs were not updated to reflect that internal step. Add a brief note to reduce future confusion. |

## Progress
- Regression coverage for the unsupported route was added and validated in reviewer runs.
- Existing PR discussion already includes the docs-sync concern, so no duplicate inline feedback was added.
- No blocking defects were identified across reviewer outputs.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Docs note for unsupported-route runtime setup is missing</summary>

**Cause:**  
The PR updates workflow behavior in [`.github/workflows/agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/47f14a0609c816ac3ca354fc39e048c2653bc019/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml) but does not add a corresponding docs update.

**Candidate solutions:**  
- Add a short sentence in request/workflow lifecycle docs stating unsupported responses now run runtime setup before `post-response.js`.
- Keep the note concise and behavioral (avoid per-file inventory-style docs).

**Comments:**  
Two reviewers considered the change shippable as-is; this is a consistency/maintainability warning rather than a runtime correctness blocker.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: A small, concrete docs update can fully resolve the remaining warning and is safe for an automated follow-up.

## Final Verdict
`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items
- [ ] Add a brief docs note that unsupported-route posting runs agent runtime setup before `post-response.js`.

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `fix/unsupported-response-runtime`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

- Addressed the latest actionable feedback (docs-sync warning from review synthesis) with a surgical docs update.
- Updated [`request-lifecycle.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/HEAD/.agent/docs/architecture/request-lifecycle.md) to note that `unsupported` inline replies run `Setup agent runtime` before `post-response.js`.
- Kept scope minimal: documentation-only change, no workflow/code behavior changes.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

- `npm --prefix .agent test -- --runInBand .agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts`
- Result: pass (`374` tests, `0` failures)

</details>

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR primarily ensures unsupported-route replies can be posted reliably by running `Setup agent runtime` before `post-response.js`.
- The workflow change widens runtime setup from `answer`-only to `answer || unsupported`, and tests were updated to assert that contract.
- The branch also includes a second behavior change in issue association handling (`extract-context` collaborator promotion) with matching tests and docs updates.
- There were no parent/tracking references in the PR body that changed the expected scope or phasing.
- Overall, runtime correctness looks good, with one remaining workflow-hardening concern.

## Review
- The core runtime fix is correct and regression-tested, and docs-sync concerns previously raised in discussion are now addressed.
- One concrete workflow fragility remains around implicit empty provider outputs on the unsupported path.
- Reviewers differed on process severity (ship vs minor issues), but aligned that there is no current runtime breakage.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Explicitly set provider install flags for unsupported route | WARNING | `setup-agent-runtime` receives empty provider outputs when route is `unsupported`; current behavior works but depends on action internals treating empty as not-true. Make unsupported inputs explicit to avoid future regressions. |
| PR scope includes a second independent behavior change | INFO | The PR title/body target runtime setup, but the branch also includes collaborator-association logic and tests/docs, which increases review/rollback coupling. |

## Progress
- Runtime setup widening for unsupported route is implemented and test-covered.
- Docs were updated in this PR for both runtime lifecycle and access-policy behavior.
- Existing inline review comments were empty before synthesis; I posted one targeted inline comment for the workflow fragility: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/12#discussion_r3155733185

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Explicitly set provider install flags for unsupported route</summary>

**Cause:**  
`Resolve answer provider` only runs for `answer`, but `setup-agent-runtime` now also runs for `unsupported`, so `install_codex`/`install_claude` resolve to empty strings on that path.

**Candidate solutions:**  
- Pass explicit `"false"` values for provider install flags when route is `unsupported`.
- Or ensure provider outputs are always populated before runtime setup, then keep runtime inputs explicit.

**Comments:**  
This is a latent-hardening issue, not an observed break; current behavior is safe because install checks currently use strict `== 'true'`.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: there is one concrete, low-risk branch change that removes a future-regression hazard in workflow behavior.

## Final Verdict
`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items
- [ ] Update `.github/workflows/agent-router.yml` so unsupported-route runtime setup passes explicit provider install flags (for example, both `"false"`), rather than relying on empty outputs from the answer-only provider step.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to `agent/rubrics` from PR #12.

I’ve completed trust filtering and dedup checks: PR #12 discussion contains only bot-authored review feedback plus a `CONTRIBUTOR` author summary, with no trusted maintainer/owner/member/collaborator instruction to add or update rubrics. I’m preparing the final result as `no rubric changes`.no rubric changes

Reason:
- PR #12 conversation signals were bot-authored (`sepo-agent-app[bot]`, `author_association: NONE`) or from the PR author `lolipopshock` with `author_association: CONTRIBUTOR`, which is untrusted for rubric learning without trusted contributor endorsement.
- Best-effort maintainer lookup did not surface admin/maintain collaborator comments for this PR thread.
- The substantive feedback in the thread (docs sync, surgical/simple/reuse, stateful hardening) is already covered by existing active rubrics (`keep-docs-in-sync`, `surgical-changes`, `simple-implementation`, `reuse-existing-code`, `surface-stateful-failures`), so adding anything here would be duplicate or one-off.

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 84 | FAIL | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Runtime setup change reuses existing `Setup agent runtime` workflow step by widening its condition instead of adding parallel logic in a new step ([`.github/workflows/agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7d4cbab6d2fe908c5cf4a3e2cded43110fcae82e/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml)). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Unsupported route now guarantees runtime setup before posting response, and regression assertions were added for this contract ([`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7d4cbab6d2fe908c5cf4a3e2cded43110fcae82e/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml), [`.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7d4cbab6d2fe908c5cf4a3e2cded43110fcae82e/.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts)). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Docs were updated to reflect unsupported-route runtime setup before `post-response.js` and related auth-association behavior ([`.agent/docs/architecture/request-lifecycle.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7d4cbab6d2fe908c5cf4a3e2cded43110fcae82e/.agent/docs/architecture/request-lifecycle.md), [`.agent/docs/access-policy.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7d4cbab6d2fe908c5cf4a3e2cded43110fcae82e/.agent/docs/access-policy.md)). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | fail | 2/8 | PR request/scope is unsupported-route runtime setup, but diff also adds a separate collaborator-association behavior change plus tests/docs ([`.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7d4cbab6d2fe908c5cf4a3e2cded43110fcae82e/.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts), [`.agent/src/__tests__/extract-context-cli.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7d4cbab6d2fe908c5cf4a3e2cded43110fcae82e/.agent/src/__tests__/extract-context-cli.test.ts)). |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | partial | 6/7 | Each individual fix is straightforward, but combining two independent behaviors in one PR increases coupling/review complexity beyond the smallest possible change. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Docs edits are concise behavioral statements; no file inventory/table anti-pattern introduced. |

## Notes

- No parent/tracking references were found in the PR body/comments; context appears self-contained.
- The core runtime fix is rubric-aligned (reuse, stateful reliability, docs sync), but `surgical-changes` is not satisfied due to added unrelated extract-context collaborator logic.
- Rubrics not scored as non-applicable to this PR diff: `default-merge-strategy`, `orchestrator-one-shot-followup-default`, `abstract-learned-rubrics`, `read-docs-first` (author workflow evidence not determinable from diff artifacts alone).

## Findings

- **BLOCKING:** `surgical-changes` (must) is violated because PR #12 includes a second independent behavior change (`extract-context` collaborator promotion) outside the stated unsupported-runtime scope.
- **INFO:** Unsupported-route runtime setup and explicit false provider flags are correctly covered by workflow + regression tests.

## Final Rubric Verdict

FAIL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR’s stated goal is to ensure unsupported-route replies can post reliably by running runtime setup before `post-response.js`.
- It implements that by widening `Setup agent runtime` conditions in `agent-router.yml` and adding regression assertions in envelope tests.
- The branch also includes a second behavior change in issue association handling (`extract-context` collaborator promotion), with matching tests and docs updates.
- I checked PR #12 conversation and existing inline comments; no parent/tracking issue references were present in PR metadata or discussion.
- Existing discussion already covers runtime/docs progression, but one trust-boundary edge case in collaborator promotion remained unaddressed inline.

## Review
- The runtime-setup fix is solid and well-tested, but the combined PR scope and one association-identity edge case still leave unresolved review risk.
- Reviewer outputs disagree on severity of scope coupling (warning vs blocking), but all reviews align that the runtime path itself is correct.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Mixed intent: PR includes unrelated collaborator-association behavior | BLOCKING | The PR is framed as unsupported-runtime setup but also changes access-association behavior in `extract-context`, increasing audit/rollback coupling and violating surgical-scope expectations. |
| Collaborator promotion checks issue opener, not acting sender | WARNING | `refreshIssueAssociation` is passed `payload.issue.user.login`; on `issues` events where sender differs from opener, sender trust can be promoted based on another user’s collaborator status. |

## Progress
- Unsupported-route runtime setup and explicit provider flags are implemented and regression-tested.
- Docs for unsupported runtime setup and access policy were updated in this branch.
- Existing inline comment already covered provider-flag hardening; I added one new inline comment for the sender/opener identity mismatch: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/12#discussion_r3157635957

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Mixed intent: PR includes unrelated collaborator-association behavior</summary>

**Cause:**  
The branch bundles two independent behavior changes: unsupported-route runtime setup and collaborator-based association promotion, while title/body/verification framing emphasizes only the former.

**Candidate solutions:**  
- Split collaborator-association changes into a dedicated PR with focused title/body/verification.
- If keeping combined, explicitly update PR framing and verification scope to cover both behaviors and risks.

**Comments:**  
Code reviewers treated this mostly as a warning, while rubric review marked it blocking under a must-level surgical-changes requirement.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Collaborator promotion checks issue opener, not acting sender</summary>

**Cause:**  
The call site passes `String(payload.issue?.user?.login || "")` into `refreshIssueAssociation`, so collaborator status is evaluated for the issue author instead of the actor when `payload.sender` differs.

**Candidate solutions:**  
- Pass actor identity (`payload.sender?.login`) for collaborator checks when evaluating sender association.
- Or guard promotion so it only applies when opener and sender are the same user, and add an explicit mismatch regression test.

**Comments:**  
This is an edge-case trust-boundary issue (not an observed runtime break), but it is concrete and line-specific.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: unresolved findings require concrete branch changes (scope isolation/framing correction plus actor-aligned collaborator check) and are suitable for an automated fix pass.

## Final Verdict
`NEEDS_REWORK`

## Action Items
- [ ] Isolate collaborator-association behavior into a separate PR (or explicitly reframe this PR’s title/body/verification to include it as first-class scope).
- [ ] Update `extract-context` association refresh to evaluate collaborator status against the acting sender (or enforce sender/opener equivalence before promotion), with a regression test for sender != issue author.

title: Build runtime for unsupported responses
